testcase: Semantic validation errors on vendored models
query:
title: Semantic validation errors on vendored models

question: Hi, for [this PR](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39746) we have some semantic validation errors- looking through the guide, it seems that a label is the only way to suppress these? I tried suppressing them in the same way I did for LintDiff errors, but it didn't seem to work. These errors are coming from models which our service passes through to AI foundry, so we don't actually own them and do not want to make changes in this folder which we are considering to be vendored from another team. (CC Johan/Travis - this is related to models from foundry/aoai)
ground_truth: The semantic validation errors you are seeing are expected false positives caused by limitations in OAV: the OpenAI/Foundry models intentionally use multi‑level discriminators, which are valid in the upstream spec but not supported by AutoRest or OAV, so the tool reports errors even though the models are correct.  In fact, the TypeSpec linter already suppresses this pattern in the upstream spec because the multiple‑discriminator design is intentional and follows the OpenAI style.  Semantic Validation does support suppressions, but only for a subset of error types due to long‑standing tooling limitations, so some errors cannot be suppressed using the same mechanism as LintDiff.  Since these model files are vendored from another team and not owned by the service, modifying them locally is not desirable; vendoring itself is allowed but carries the usual risks of keeping copies in sync.  At the moment, the only officially supported way to share TSP code is through npm packages, but the relevant packages are not yet in a consumable state, so vendoring plus suppression remains the practical short‑term approach until a stable package becomes available.  If needed, the Azure SDK tooling team can enable permanent suppressions for these specific Semantic Validation errors.

testcase: Getting flagged for Avocado check during Typespec migration
query:
title: Getting flagged for Avocado check during Typespec migration

question: Hello,
 
I am working on migrating the servicefabric resource-manager specs to Typespec. 
[\[servicefabric\] Typespec migration by iliu816 · Pull Request #39844 · Azure/azure-rest-api-specs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39844)
 
I am seeing failed Avocado checks for paths that have been moved to the servicefabricmanagedclusters folder. I added a comment in the PR. Based on the docs, I believe I need a PR reviewer to add the Avocado-approved tag to this PR to neutralize the failing check? Please let me know if I am misunderstanding the error.
ground_truth:
You are correct in your understanding. The Avocado check is failing because it detects paths (such as /subscriptions/{}/resourcegroups/{}/providers/microsoft.servicefabric/managedclusters/*) that have been moved to the servicefabricmanagedclusters folder, but the tool may not yet fully recognize the migration or folder refactor. This is a known issue during migration and refactoring, especially when paths are moved between folders or services.
To resolve the failing Avocado check and unblock your PR, you need a PR reviewer to apply the Approved-Avocado label to your PR. This label will neutralize the Avocado error and allow the PR to proceed. This is the recommended workaround until the tool is updated to better handle these migration scenarios.
If you are unsure whether your service qualifies for a one-time suppression, you can also reach out to azversioning@service.microsoft.com for guidance. For more details, see the Uniform Versioning Violation Guide and the Avocado Known Issues documentation.
In summary: Yes, you need a PR reviewer to add the Approved-Avocado label to your PR to resolve the failing Avocado check in this migration scenario.
References
[Known Issues | Azure Rest Api Specs Wiki](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/wiki/Swagger-Avocado)
[API-Spec-Review-FAQ.md | Azure Rest Api Specs Wiki](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/wiki/API-Spec-Review-FAQ)

expected_references.title: Known Issues | Azure Rest Api Specs Wiki
API-Spec-Review-FAQ.md | Azure Rest Api Specs Wiki

testcase: build failures
query:
title: build failures

question: I've created this PR for adding the new SRP version (2025-08-01) in swagger but facing a few [issues - Add StorageConnector and StorageDataShare resources with 2025-08-01 version by ujjawaljain-msft](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39307)
1. Swagger LintDiff is failing as [ProvisioningStateMustBeReadOnly](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/actions/runs/21397558065). We have the right model/use present for provisioningState but still getting this error. I think this was overwritten in previous PRs.
2. [.NET SDK Generation failure](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39307/checks?check_run_id=61644607615) - The detailed error doesn't mention anything regarding the failure.
3. [Python SDK Generation failure](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39307/checks?check_run_id=61644690304) - This fails with "We have already generated a schema with name ErrorResponse". I suspect this is due to the fact that we use the ErrorResponse defined in the ARM's common-types but SRP also has a ErrorResponse model, causing conflict. Is there a way to override this for python SDK? (Tried a couple of things but nothing seems to resolve the issue)
Could you please help with the above-mentioned issues?
ground_truth:
1. To suppress Swagger LintDiff errors that were not introduced by updates in your pull request, you should use a permanent suppression. This is preferred to avoid repeatedly addressing the same issue in future PRs. The suppression should be added using the AutoRest extension suppression mechanism, typically by editing or creating a suppression directive in your spec folder (often in a file like suppression.yaml).
Here’s how to do it:
Identify the exact error code and the JSON path to the element causing the error. You can find this in the raw log for the LintDiff check, which will include a jsonpath array pointing to the problematic element.
Convert the jsonpath array to a string suitable for the suppression's where clause (e.g., $.paths["/resource/path"].get.responses["200"]).
Add a suppression entry in your suppression file, scoped as narrowly as possible to the specific error instance. For example:
- code: LintDiffErrorCode
  from: YourSpecFile.json
  where: $.paths["/your/path"].get.responses["200"]
  reason: "Error not introduced by this PR; legacy issue."
Commit this suppression to your PR. Once reviewed and approved, the error will be marked as neutral and not block your PR.
Permanent suppressions are preferred for errors not introduced by your changes. For more details and examples, see the Azure REST API specs documentation and wiki links provided.
2. For .NET SDK generation, there is no csharp emitter in azure-rest-api-specs/specification/storage/Storage.Management/tspconfig.yaml at 5fed9ed058e95aefced… So, there is no generation at all. And .NET SDK validation is optional for your PR, can just ignore it
3. Made [a commit](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39307/changes/4b4c9180cca826f8981e327a892c50780ca130a5) just now to fix python failure.

testcase: .net build failing
query:
title: .net build failing

question: My PR shows dotnet build failing for unrelated files [Swagger changes for Vwan Express Route Gateway Failover and Insights by shubhika1795 · Pull Request…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39841)
Please can someone help me with this.
ground_truth:
You are seeing .NET SDK build failures for files unrelated to your changes because the SDK generation pipelines in the azure-rest-api-specs repo run against the entire service directory (in this case, 'network/resource-manager/Microsoft.Network'), not just the files you modified in your PR. This means that any existing issues in other files within the same service directory can cause your PR checks to fail, even if your changes are correct.
To resolve this:
Review the detailed error logs from the .NET SDK generation pipeline. These logs will specify which files and issues are causing the failures.
If the errors are truly unrelated to your changes, you have two options:
Fix the issues in the unrelated files as part of your PR (preferred, as it helps keep the repo healthy).
If you cannot fix them (e.g., you don't own those files), you can reach out to the owners of those files or service areas to coordinate a fix. Sometimes, you may need to wait for those issues to be resolved before your PR can pass.
General SDK generation check failures cannot be suppressed; only breaking changes can be suppressed with approval. If the failure is due to a breaking change, you must follow the breaking change review process.
In summary, the build failures are a result of the repo's validation scope, and you will need to address or coordinate fixes for any issues in the service directory, not just your own changes.

testcase: SDK Breaking Change review
query:
title: SDK Breaking Change review

question: Hello! Would it be possible to get the SDK breaking change approvals on this PR? We are looking to get this completed ASAP to move forward with our version release as we have important deadlines coming up.
https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/38470
ground_truth:
Based on the current status of your PR (https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/38470), SDK breaking change review is required for Go and JavaScript SDKs, as indicated by the labels 'BreakingChange-Go-Sdk' and 'BreakingChange-JavaScript-Sdk'. According to the Azure spec PR workflow, SDK reviewers will proactively evaluate the breaking changes after ARM review and other required steps are completed. You do not need to take additional action besides waiting for SDK reviewers to complete their evaluation, which typically takes around two business days after the previous steps are done.
If your PR contains breaking changes that cannot be mitigated, you should add the necessary suppressions (for example, in 'sdk-suppressions.yaml' for Go SDK) as described in the Azure REST API specs wiki. Once suppressions are added, SDK reviewers will review them and, if approved, will add the corresponding approval labels (e.g., 'BreakingChange-Go-Sdk-Suppression-Approved').
To expedite the process:
Ensure all validation errors are addressed or suppressed as appropriate.
Add any required suppression files for SDK breaking changes.
Once all other checks are green and suppressions are in place, the SDK reviewers will proceed with their review.
If you have urgent deadlines, you may leave a comment in the PR to notify SDK reviewers of your timeline, but the process itself is automated and follows the workflow described above.

testcase: PR review for Datamap api doc fix
query:
title: PR review for Datamap api doc fix

question: I have raised a PR to add missing information (an optional parameter) to datamap api doc. The Swagger BreakingChange is failing as it is asking for a version upgrade as well, which we don't want since it's just an existing parameter which was missed in docs.

Can someone please review it and suggest a way to override this failed check?
 
https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39983
ground_truth:
You cannot override or suppress the Swagger BreakingChange validation failure for adding a new parameter—even if it is optional—without upgrading the API version. According to the Azure Versioning and Breaking Changes Policy, any change to a published API version (including adding optional parameters) is considered a contract change and is not allowed, as it can break existing clients or create confusion about which version of the API is in use. The only exceptions are for bug fixes that correct the spec to match actual service behavior, and these require explicit approval and labeling (e.g., 'BreakingChange-Approved-BugFix').
For your scenario (adding a missing optional parameter to the documentation for a published API version), you have two options:
Upgrade the API version: The recommended and compliant approach is to introduce a new API version (e.g., '2024-XX-XX') that includes the new parameter. This ensures customers can opt into the new contract and avoids breaking changes for existing clients.
Request a breaking change review: If you believe this change is a bug fix (i.e., the service has always supported this parameter and the spec was simply incorrect), you may request approval from the Breaking Change Review Board. If approved, you can apply the 'BreakingChange-Approved-BugFix' label to your PR. However, this is only allowed if the change is strictly correcting the spec to match the actual service behavior, not introducing new functionality.
You cannot simply suppress the Swagger BreakingChange check for this type of change. Please follow the process at https://aka.ms/brch for breaking change review if you believe your case qualifies as a bug fix. Otherwise, you must create a new API version.

testcase: Getting BreakingChange-Python-SDK label
query:
title: Getting BreakingChange-Python-SDK label

question: Hi, we are releasing a GA API version and removing the "-preview". There are no changes for this stable API version from the previous preview version. However, we are getting the BreakingChange-Python-Sdk label in the PR (not for any other languages though). Does anyone know why this might be happening?
 
[Add api version 2026-03-15 for Disconnected Operations by christinekc · Pull Request #26674 · Azure…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/26674)
ground_truth:
You are seeing the BreakingChange-Python-Sdk label on your PR for releasing a GA API version (removing '-preview') even though there are no changes from the previous preview version, and other language SDKs do not show this label. This is likely due to how the SDK breaking change detection tooling works for Python specifically.
According to Azure API versioning policy, when you release a new GA version by simply removing the '-preview' suffix and making no other changes, this should not be considered a breaking change for SDKs, as the contract remains identical. The tooling is supposed to compare the new GA spec with the previous preview spec and only flag breaking changes if there are actual contract differences.
However, in practice, the Python SDK breaking change check sometimes triggers a false positive in this scenario. This can happen due to differences in how the Python SDK generator interprets versioning or due to metadata differences between preview and GA folders, even if the OpenAPI contract is unchanged. Other language SDKs may not be as sensitive to these differences, which is why you only see the label for Python.
To resolve this:
Double-check that the OpenAPI files for the new GA and previous preview versions are byte-for-byte identical except for the version string and folder name.
If they are identical, this is a known tooling quirk. You can request a suppression for the Python SDK breaking change label as described in the Suppression cases table. The label to request is BreakingChange-Python-Sdk-Suppression-Approved.
No action is needed for other languages if they are not flagged.
This situation does not indicate a real breaking change if the contract is unchanged. The suppression process exists for exactly these cases.

This is the relevant discussion on the changes made in that PR: [Josephine Qiu: Kirstyn Joy Amperiadis I moved your question here | Azure SDK > TypeSpec Discussion | Microsoft Teams](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/message/19:906c1efbbec54dc8949ac736633e6bdf@thread.skype/1770606658256?tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47&groupId=3e17dcb0-4257-4a30-b843-77f47f1d4121&parentMessageId=1770606658256&teamName=Azure%20SDK&channelName=TypeSpec%20Discussion&createdTime=1770606658256)
 
Another follow up aside from the ARM team's review - Renhe Li / Chenjie Shi can you please review and add the BreakingChange-Go-SDK-Approved tag if you think the PR is good on that end?
ground_truth:
Based on the information provided, the PR '[Web] Fix encode for binary response' (https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/40055) currently has the 'BreakingChange-Go-Sdk' label, which means it introduces a breaking change for the Go SDK. According to Azure SDK policy, any breaking change for an SDK must be reviewed and either mitigated or explicitly suppressed and approved by SDK reviewers.
To proceed:
Renhe Li or Chenjie Shi (as Go SDK reviewers) should review the PR to determine if the breaking change is acceptable and cannot be avoided.
If they agree the change is necessary and the suppression is justified, they should approve the suppression. Once approved, the 'BreakingChange-Go-Sdk-Approved' label will be added to the PR, allowing it to proceed to the next stage of the review process.
The PR author has already requested this action in the Teams channel, which is the correct process. The SDK reviewers are expected to proactively review such suppressions within approximately 48 business hours. No further action is needed from the PR author except to wait for the SDK reviewers' decision or respond to any feedback they provide.

testcase: SuppressionReviewRequired mistakenly added?
query:
title: SuppressionReviewRequired mistakenly added?

question: Hi all, we have this PR out for review, and the SuppressionReviewRequired tag was added, however no suppressions are added in this PR. Was this added by mistake, and is this a bug that needs to be fixed?
 
[Added Standard as new enum value for Security type starting 2025-11-01 api version by bhnayyar · Pu…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39957)
ground_truth:
The SuppressionReviewRequired tag on PR https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/39957 was not necessarily added by mistake, even if no new suppressions are present in the PR. The automation for this label is triggered whenever a PR modifies suppressions, adds new suppressions, or sometimes when it detects changes that may require suppression review (such as certain breaking change or versioning issues). However, there are cases where the label can be added due to automation logic, even if the PR does not actually introduce new suppressions.
If your PR does not add or modify suppressions, and you believe the label was added in error, you can:
Double-check that no suppressions.yaml or sdk-suppressions.yaml files were changed in the PR.
Confirm that no new suppressions were added for validation tools (TypeSpec, LintDiff, Avocado, etc).
If confirmed, you can comment in the PR to request removal of the label, explaining that no suppressions were added or modified.
In summary, the label can sometimes be added due to automation logic, but if no suppressions are present, it can be safely removed after confirmation.

Based on the PR content and comments, your ConfidentialLedger resource-manager stable-2026-02-24 API spec update is following the current Azure REST API specification guidelines:
Folder Structure: The spec files have been moved to the correct directory structure: specification/confidentialledger/resource-manager/Microsoft.ConfidentialLedger/ConfidentialLedger/. This aligns with the recommended pattern for ARM services, where the Resource Provider Namespace (RPNS) is used as a subfolder under resource-manager, and each service gets its own folder. This fixes the previously deprecated pattern of placing specs under Confidentialledger.Management.
TypeSpec Usage: The new stable API spec is authored in TypeSpec, which is the recommended approach for new and updated Azure services. The .tsp files are present and organized as expected.
Managed CCF Removal: The obsolete Managed CCF references have been removed from the new stable API spec. The ManagedCCF.tsp file is marked with @removed(Versions.v2026_02_23), which is the correct way to indicate removal in TypeSpec for a stable version. This ensures backward compatibility for preview versions while removing it from the stable spec.
Examples Handling: The examples are present both in the source examples/ folder and as generated output under stable/{version}/examples/. This is standard convention: source examples are maintained for TypeSpec, and generated examples are emitted for OpenAPI and downstream tooling. There is no actual duplication—this is intentional and required for tooling compatibility.
API Versioning: The new stable API version follows the correct YYYY-MM-DD format (2026-02-24). Preview and stable versions are separated as required.
Breaking Change Handling: The PR is labeled with BreakingChangeReviewRequired and BreakingChange-Approved-BugFix, indicating that the removal of Managed CCF has gone through the proper breaking change review process. This is required for any breaking change in stable versions.
CI and Review Process: The PR is labeled for ARM review and has the PublishToCustomers label, indicating readiness for publication. Ensure all CI checks are passing before merging.
Summary: Your PR is compliant with Azure REST API guidelines for management-plane specs. The folder structure, TypeSpec usage, example handling, versioning, and breaking change process are all correct. Before merging, confirm that all required CI checks are green and that the ARM review board has approved the breaking change. No further structural corrections are needed based on the current repo standards.
If you need additional review for API shape, naming, or schema details, please provide the actual TypeSpec or OpenAPI content for deeper analysis.

testcase: Need PR review for https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/40657
query:
title: Need PR review for https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/40657

question: Could you please approve the breaking change in the PR [\[ConfidentialLedger\]Add stable version 2026-02-23 for ConfidentialLedger and ConfidentialLedgerIden…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/40657)?
This update promotes the 2024-12-09-preview specification to the new stable version 2026-02-23. The version has been safely used in production for over a year. Thank you.
ground_truth:
The API spec team cannot approve the breaking change in PR 40657 solely based on the fact that the promoted version has been safely used in production for over a year. According to the Azure Versioning and Breaking Changes Policy, any breaking change—regardless of prior production usage—must go through the formal breaking change review and approval process. This includes:
Submitting a breaking-change proposal for review by the Azure REST API Stewardship Board.
Receiving explicit approval before the change can be merged.
Ensuring customer notification and migration timelines are followed if the change impacts users.
Your PR is correctly flagged with the BreakingChangeReviewRequired label, which means it is blocked until the breaking change review process is completed. Please follow the instructions in the "Next Steps to Merge" comment and the guidance at [aka.ms/brch]. Only after the review board approves the breaking change (and applies the appropriate approval label) can the PR proceed.
Prior production usage does not exempt the change from this policy. If the change is a bug fix or has been previously approved, you may apply the relevant approval label (e.g., BreakingChange-Approved-BugFix or BreakingChange-Approved-Previously) if applicable, but this must be justified and documented.

ground_truth:
You have provided the Swagger ApiDocPreview link for your PR (#27157), but it appears that the preview does not show any Swagger documentation for your changes. This can happen for several reasons:
The ApiDocPreview pipeline may not have onboarded your Swagger files correctly, or there may be a build issue preventing the preview from being generated.
Sometimes, the preview link is available but does not display the expected Swagger content due to errors in the spec or the onboarding process.
According to Azure API Spec Review guidance, the ApiDocPreview check is not a required validation for PR merging. If the preview is missing or broken, you can:
Ignore the failure if you do not need the documentation preview for your workflow.
If you need the preview and the error message is unclear, follow the troubleshooting steps:
Go to the SiteHelp Portal.
Submit a request under 'Content authoring and publishing' > 'Reference CI Jobs'.
Provide details about the missing Swagger preview and your PR link.
For most cases, you can proceed with your PR review and merging as long as all other required CI checks are passing. The ApiDocPreview is only for documentation purposes and does not block the PR.
If you want to manually preview your Swagger, you can use the VS Code extension '42Crunch.vscode-openapi' to load your OpenAPI spec locally and review the documentation.
you can also try asking here:[ Documentation | Azure SDK | Microsoft Teams](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/channel/19%3A9662dea83b9f494aa4b76fed5d20acaf%40thread.skype/Documentation?groupId=3e17dcb0-4257-4a30-b843-77f47f1d4121&tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47)

testcase: RenamedFrom & Copilot Review
ground_truth:
The pattern using `@renamedFrom(Versions.v2026_05_01_preview, "reimage")` + `@removed(Versions.v2026_05_01_preview)` on the old operation, combined with `@added(Versions.v2026_05_01_preview)` + `@sharedRoute` on the new operation, is correct for the stated goal. `@renamedFrom` specifies the version at which the rename takes effect — before that version, the effective name is "reimage"; at that version, the old operation is removed and the new one (`reimage` with the new body) takes over. The `reimageOld` symbol name is just a TypeSpec symbol to avoid a naming collision in the same interface and does not surface to clients.

For a more definitive human answer, ask in the TypeSpec Discussion channel and keep pinging until you get a human response.

testcase: SDK Validation CI failures for both Java and Python
query:
title: SDK Validation CI failures for both Java and Python

question: We're seeing SDK Validation CI failures for both Java and Python on the following PR:
PR Link - [TLS 1.3 added in 2025-05-preview version by schaudhari6254888 · Pull Request #41229 · Azure/azure-rest-api-specs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41229)

ground_truth:
There are two distinct issues: (1) The `Swagger BreakingChange` (`AddedEnumValue` + `NoVersionChange`) failure occurs because adding `"1.3"` to the TLS version enum within the same api-version is a breaking change. testcase: Breaking change for preview version
query:
title: Breaking change for preview version

question: I have a question about the exception process for preview API versions. Our team has an open PR to update the `2025-08-01-preview` API version. The changes we're adding affect a new, currently-unreleased endpoint. We're trying to update the API spec for public preview of the new feature, but the PR had the "VersioningReviewRequired" label applied to it. Since these changes are for an unreleased API that customers haven't begun using yet, is there a process to get an exception for the PR? Do I need to attend office hours for the exception, or can it be done asynchronously?
PR Link: [Add Validate API, new parameters/statuses for Redis Migration by sharedferret · Pull Request #41386](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41386)
ground_truth:
Once any API version (including preview) is merged into `main`, it is treated as published, and in-place changes trigger `VersioningReviewRequired`. Two options: (1) Preferred — create a new preview api-version (e.g., `2025-09-01-preview`) with the new endpoint, which avoids the need for an exception entirely. (2) Request an exception asynchronously via the breaking change/versioning process at `aka.ms/brch` — no need to attend office hours. The board can grant approval labels like `Versioning-Approved-Benign` asynchronously on the PR.

testcase: Help with a suppression review
query:
title: Help with a suppression review

question: PR: [Typespec changes for 2024-04-01-preview SDK release by ransinha1989 · Pull Request #41971 · Azure/a…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41971).
 
Need some help with the suppression for UnsupportedPatchProperties. It seems to me that removing top-level ARM properties (id, type, name) from the PATCH payload should not be a cross-version breaking SDK change. The PR author is concerned that it is breaking and will require back-end API changes, but I'm advocating for removing those properties from the model and removing the suppression.
 
Mark Cowlishaw, Mike Harder, and/or Josephine Qiu, can you comment?
ground_truth:
Removing `id`, `name`, and `type` from the PATCH request model is the correct fix and should be treated as a spec bugfix, not a breaking change requiring backend changes. Per ARM guidance, PATCH bodies should only contain updatable properties — top-level ARM fields are server-populated and must not be sent in PATCH requests. The solution is to create separate models for the PATCH operation where these fields are marked as readonly, rather than suppressing the validation. The PR author confirmed this approach works: creating new PATCH-specific models for endpoint and profile where `id`, `name`, and `type` are readonly, which eliminates the need for suppressions without touching existing code.

testcase: Help needed - PR tagged as VersioningReviewRequired, but API version is behind AFEC and not shipped to any customer
query:
title: Help needed - PR tagged as VersioningReviewRequired, but API version is behind AFEC and not shipped to any customer

question: Hi, we are working on a new API version `2026-03-15-preview` for our RP Microsoft.Sovereign. This version introduces a new resource type SovereignView. From its inception, it is behind an AFEC feature flag and that feature is *not* exposed to any customers so far.
 
I have raised a PR [Add support for MSI to the SovereignView resource by shaaradmsft · Pull Request #28108 · Azure/azur…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/28108) to add System Assigned Managed Identity support to this resource type under the same API version `2026-03-15-preview`, due to which it has been flagged for `VersioningReviewRequired` tag.
 
I can attest that there is no customer impact or breakages due to this change. How can I proceed with getting reviews and approvals for this PR?
ground_truth:
Even though the feature is AFEC-gated with no customer exposure, this is still a typical breaking change to an existing API version and must follow the standard breaking-change/versioning review process. Go to https://aka.ms/brch and submit a breaking-change request referencing your PR and the API version, then apply the appropriate `Versioning-Approved-*` label (e.g., `Versioning-Approved-PrivatePreview`) once approved.

Note that merging the spec into RPSaaSMaster does not automatically change ARM runtime behavior. The specs repo is a description of the API surface for tooling/SDKs/docs; ARM's actual validation behavior is controlled by the RP implementation and ARM registration/manifest. After merging, you'll still need the normal RP-side rollout and any required ARM manifest/registration updates before the new `identity` property is accepted in real requests.

testcase: Breaking Change related to ARM review request
query:
title: Breaking Change related to ARM review request

question: As part of the feedback from the ARM review for [Microsoft.NetworkCloud 2026-05-01-preview API by bryan-strassner · Pull Request #42234 · Azure/azur…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/42234), it was identified:
 
My question is forward-looking: is there a plan (tracked work item) to introduce a proper PATCH-specific model in a future API version -- one where nested properties also have no required fields? The current schema forces clients to re-send values they may not want to touch, which undermines the purpose of PATCH.

I have submitted a PR to the private repo for our upcoming, approved, but not yet actually delivered stable API: [Microsoft.NetworkCloud 2026-07-01 revise for public repo feedback by bryan-strassner · Pull Request…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/28145).
 
Of course, it recognizes a breaking change. I'm seeking advice on path forward. I know that I will need to request ARM breaking change approval, but this also indicates SDK breaking changes.
ground_truth:
A few recommendations:
- Unless they need to be bundled, move the deletion of the old preview and the addition of the new stable into separate PRs to simplify things.
- Address the failing TypeSpec Validation check first; until that passes, all other check results are suspect.
- For breaking changes (both within an existing version and cross-version), follow the standard documented processes — there is no special path for this scenario.

Note that the SDK breaking change pipeline was temporarily disabled in the private repo to help GitHub resolve an outage on their side. It was re-enabled in the public repo but not yet in the private repo, so SDK breaking change failures may not surface there until the team finishes re-enabling it.

testcase: Rest API Spec PR Persistent Error (ProvisioningStateMustBeReadOnly)
query:
title: Rest API Spec PR Persistent Error (ProvisioningStateMustBeReadOnly)

question: Hello, 
 
I have a PR to modify the rest api spec: [[Microsoft.Web] Introduce new api version "2026-06-01-preview" and new resource type "Microsoft.Web…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/42584)
that I have been resolving errors as they are detected by the validation checks for, but it seems to be stuck on an error stating that a new property of some of my resources, called ProvisioningState, must be read-only in the openapi.json. However when checking the file, it is marked as such, so I am not sure why the error persists.

But that also leads to changes in the openapi spec of previous api versions different than the one i am introducing. Is that okay?
ground_truth:
Yes, it is acceptable — and preferred — to check in the regenerated OpenAPI for older API versions in the same PR when fixing a TypeSpec emitter configuration issue like `use-read-only-status-schema: true`. You should have set this option initially, but correcting it now and accepting the (very minor) breaking change is the right approach. The fix should be made in the TypeSpec sources / `tspconfig.yaml`, then run `npx tsp compile` and commit all regenerated OpenAPI files.

For questions about whether the ARM manifest needs to be updated for older API versions that received the OpenAPI fix, contact the ARM team or post in the TypeSpec Discussion channel.

testcase: New check ARMModelingReviewRequired?
query:
title: New check ARMModelingReviewRequired?

question: We got an open PR in draft stage for a new api version, open for a couple of days now and have been added some things to it every now and then. Until today all expected checks have been passing. But since a new commit today it suddenly shows a new label **ARMModelingReviewRequired**
 
Error: Microsoft.CloudHealth: new resource types detected without a valid ARM lease
Error: New resource types detected without a valid ARM lease. Please schedule a discussion at ARM API Modeling Office Hours before merging:
 
But we are not adding any new resource type in this PR?! Is this a false positive or what am I missing?
 
Thanks!
 
PR [https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41963](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/41963)
ground_truth:
This is a new check recently added by the ARM team that enforces the ARM lease model for resource types. For existing RPs, a 90-day lease was permitted to prevent excessive office hours discussions, but a few RPs were missed. The owner can add a lease for your RP to unblock you. If your PR truly does not add any new resource types (e.g., it only adds new POST actions on existing RTs), it is a false positive in the new check, and the check owner will investigate.

testcase: Review for a new API release
query:
title: Review for a new API release

question: Hi API Spec Review,
I need a review for this PR [Add SandboxGroup API to Microsoft.ContainerInstance TypeSpec by iyyappam · Pull Request #42804 · Az…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/42804)
 
I am getting a breaking change error for a component which is already released. What is the workaround this [Add SandboxGroup API to Microsoft.ContainerInstance TypeSpec · Azure/azure-rest-api-specs@7eda2bb](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/actions/runs/25242005865)
 
Also, there are a few lint error, which is again due to an existing component.
[Add SandboxGroup API to Microsoft.ContainerInstance TypeSpec · Azure/azure-rest-api-specs@7eda2bb](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/actions/runs/25242005867)
ground_truth:
The Breaking Change crash is a tool bug ([openapi-diff#479](https://github.com/Azure/openapi-diff/issues/479)) caused by your TypeSpec model named `Resource` conflicting with a name in `common-types/types.json`. The fix is to rename the TSP model `Resource` to something that does not conflict — even if this means going through breaking change approval, it's much better than having a spec that the breaking change tooling can't even analyze. PRs should never be merged while breaking changes is crashing, and applying a `BreakingChange-Approved-*` label is not the correct resolution.

For the LintDiff `ProvisioningStateMustBeReadOnly` errors, follow the FAQ guidance at [TypeSpec Azure - Status read-only error](https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/docs/troubleshoot/status-read-only-error/). Most instances should be fixed rather than suppressed. If a fix would itself cause additional breaking changes, you can add suppressions in `suppressions.yaml` with an explanation of why keeping things invalid is preferable to taking a one-time breaking change to get compliant. For TypeSpec/`ProvisioningStateMustBeReadOnly` specifics, Mark Cowlishaw is the expert; for spec conversion follow-ups, Janine Zhang and Pan Shao can help.

---

testcase: What needs to be done for this PR to merge?
query:
title: What needs to be done for this PR to merge?

question: Hello All, 
I have this PR [Minimal changes for 2024-04-01-preview API version with suppressions by ransinha1989 · Pull Request…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/42647)
 
I already have "ARMSignedOff", "Versioning-Approved-BranchPolicyException" and "PublishToCustomers" tags added to it.
 
Could someone please explain this error to me?
[image]
ground_truth:
These are intra-version Swagger BreakingChange errors (rules 1006 RemovedDefinition, 1017 ReferenceRedirection, 1029 ReadonlyPropertyChanged) that are not bypassed by the `ARMSignedOff` / `Versioning-Approved-BranchPolicyException` / `PublishToCustomers` labels — those address ARM review and branch-policy versioning, not the concrete diff inside the same api-version. To resolve, either make the swagger logically equivalent to the existing 2024-04-01-preview (no removed definitions, no changed `$ref`, no `readOnly` flips), or — if the differences are intentional — go through the breaking-change process at [aka.ms/brch](https://aka.ms/brch) and obtain the appropriate breaking-change approval label, or introduce a new api-version instead of mutating the existing one.

testcase: Unknown type
query:
title: Unknown type

question: Hello 
 
I'm working on a adding a new API preview version with one new feature: [Key Vault 2026-03-01-preview API version by melissamserv · Pull Request #42905 · Azure/azure-rest-a…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/42905)
 
We have the `metadata` field that is a JSON object, so I think the type `unknown` or `Record<unkwon>` is a better fit in this case. We chose this approach instead of a well-defined structure to avoid exposing these fields to 3Ps. When I try to use the `unknown` type I get a warning:

Azure services must not have properties of type `unknown`.TypeSpec(@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core/no-unknown)

or

PlatformManaged.metadata should not use ': Record<unknown>'. Use ': Record<string>' instead.TypeSpec[@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core/bad-record-type](https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/docs/libraries/azure-core/rules/bad-record-type)

 
Do you know how we should handle this?
 
Thanks!
ground_truth:
This question is better asked in the TypeSpec Discussion channel. For the technical guidance: both `unknown` and a bare `Record<unknown>` are intentionally discouraged. If the metadata values are always strings, use `metadata?: Record<string>` — this satisfies the `bad-record-type` and `no-unknown` rules. If you truly need arbitrary JSON, define a wrapper model that spreads `...Record<unknown>` and add targeted `#suppress` directives for `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core/no-unknown` and `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-core/bad-record-type` on that model with a clear justification. TypeSpec will emit this as `type: object` + `additionalProperties` in OpenAPI, which is the accepted equivalent.

testcase: ARM RPC Rule breaking : TopLevelResourcesListBySubscription
ground_truth:
LintDiff is flagging `RecommendationsPatchPayload` because it extends `Azure.ResourceManager.Foundations.Resource` and is used on a resource-shaped subscription-level path, so the validator infers it is a new top-level ARM resource and applies `RPC-Get-V1-04`/`RPC-Get-V1-05`. The check started failing because the pipeline picked up newer validator rules and/or a newer base after your rebase. The fix is to change `RecommendationsPatchPayload` so it no longer extends `Azure.ResourceManager.Foundations.Resource` — model it as a plain (non-resource) payload for the PATCH body/response, keep the underlying recommendation as the actual ARM resource, regenerate the OpenAPI, and rerun the PR. Avoid adding GET/list operations for the patch payload, and prefer correcting the modeling over requesting a scoped LintDiff suppression. (See related duplicate thread in TypeSpec Discussion.)

testcase: PR validation failing due to RP not present in private repo (it is already merged there)
query:
title: PR validation failing due to RP not present in private repo (it is already merged there)

question: Hi, I need some help unblocking my PR. [Moving Napster from private repo to public repo by nikhgup · Pull Request #43259 · Azure/azure-rest…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/43259)
Our RP (`Napster`) already exists in the private repo [(`azure-rest-api-specs-pr/specification/napster`)](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/tree/RPSaaSMaster/specification/napster) but follows the v1 folder structure. While moving to the public repo, v2 folder structure appears to be mandatory, so I updated it in the public repo.
However, validation is failing with `CI-RpaaSRPNotInPrivateRepo`, saying the RP is not present in the private repo and treating it as a new RP. I suspect this is happening because of the v1 → v2 folder structure change, even though this is an existing RP.
What would be the recommended way to unblock this PR? Is there any exception/process for existing RPs moving from private to public repo?
ground_truth:
Do not combine a merge from private to public and a migration from fsv1 to fsv2 in a single PR. The optimal sequence is to convert to fsv2 in RPSaaSMaster first, then merge to public/main; alternatively merge fsv1 to public/main first and convert to fsv2 later. The `CI-RpaaSRPNotInPrivateRepo` label can be ignored — it stopped working when security was ramped up (see [issue #36717](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/issues/36717)).

If timelines require keeping fsv1, merging the existing spec to public/main as fsv1 is acceptable; the fsv2 conversion can be done later (by you or by the Azure SDK team). To get past the TypeSpec Validation `FolderStructure / MustUseV2` check in that case, add a suppression to the spec folder, e.g.:

```
- tool: TypeSpecValidation
  rules: [FolderStructure]
  sub-rules: [MustUseV2]
  reason: Specs added as folder structure v1, not yet converted to v2
  paths:
    - <YourSpec>.Management
```

See [specification/migrate/suppressions.yaml](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/blob/main/specification/migrate/suppressions.yaml) for an example. Qiaoqiao Zhang is the expert on fsv2; Pan Shao owns TSP conversions. Naming and folder structures should also be discussed with the ARM (or data-plane) review board early since the repos don't handle moves/renames well.

testcase: Transition from Private Preview to Public Preview Version
query:
title: Transition from Private Preview to Public Preview Version

question: Hi API Spec Review,
We currently have two **private preview (PrPr)** API versions for our RP under **Microsoft.ZeroTrustSegmentation** in RPSaaSMaster.

We also have **another private preview version** that we would like to eventually replace the existing APIs with.
The new version introduces **breaking changes** compared to the existing PrPr versions, and our end goal is to have **a single version in the spec**.
**Note:** We currently have **no customers** - neither internal Microsoft customers nor external customers.
 
**Current status:**

- We're in the process of removing all resources that use the PrPr versions - should be completed by **end of week**
- We've also **blocked creation of new resources** using those versions

What's the recommended approach for progressing the spec?

- Add the new version alongside the PrPr versions (which we already [opened](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/28501)), and remove PrPr in a follow-up PR
- Remove PrPr and add the new version in the same PR - if so, what are the implications regarding RP registrations? For example, should we first roll out registration with no API versions (if that's even possible), then merge the new spec, and finally roll out registration with the new API?
ground_truth:
Add the new API version in one PR, then remove the old API versions in a second PR. The repo checks handle this much better, and in general it's best to change a single API version per PR.

testcase: List operation does not support pagination
query:
title: List operation does not support pagination

question: Hello, we are currently investigating a bug observed during list operations from our Horizon DB Swagger. First request returns 200 with content, but then we see 502 error appear for the collection response. We believe there was a bug introduced that the list API supports pagination when in actuality it does not. 

 
We are trying to determine best way to resolve this bug and wanted confirmation if setting nextLinkName to null as shown in this PR would help: [Set nextLinkName to null on pageable responses since pagination is not supported yet by nachoalonso…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/42760)
 
Neha Rao for awareness
ground_truth:
List operations are required to be pageable, and if a non-pageable list operation were made pageable later, that would be a breaking change. The response shown is the service actively returning an invalid `nextLink`, which is a service-side bug, not a spec issue. The implementation needs to be robust over paged responses (it's common for paged responses to contain only one page of data). If the behavior is coming from default RPaaS handling, contact the RPaaS team; otherwise, take it to the ARM API Review Board (armapireview@microsoft.com / [ARM API Wiki](https://armwiki.azurewebsites.net/rp_onboarding/process/api_review.html)).

testcase: PR Review Required
query:
title: PR Review Required

question: Hi API Spec Review,
 
I need review for this PR.
[Add 2026-05-01-preview API version for Vnet support by rsinghal26 · Pull Request #43345 · Azure/azu…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/43345)
 
LintDiff validation is failing with false positive error as provisioningState is already marked as readOnly. I need help to supress this validation.
Violation: file:///home/runner/work/azure-rest-api-specs/azure-rest-api-specs/after/specification/confluent/resource-manager/Microsoft.Confluent/preview/2026-05-01-preview/confluent.json:2508

Level: error

Code: ProvisioningStateMustBeReadOnly

Message: provisioningState property must be set to readOnly.
ground_truth:
For `ProvisioningStateMustBeReadOnly` on TypeSpec-generated specs, the recommended fix is to enable the `use-read-only-status-schema: true` option on the autorest emitter (see [TypeSpec emitter usage](https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/docs/emitters/typespec-autorest/reference/emitter/#use-read-only-status-schema)) and re-emit the OpenAPI. This may add a `readOnly: true` flag directly on the `provisioningState` model across existing swagger files — that is acceptable and a reviewer can sign off on that as a non-functional accuracy fix even though it triggers the breaking-change flag, since the only change is marking already read-only status as read-only. It's better to be accurate than to worry about process. After regenerating, get the breaking-change label re-applied (and reach out to language-specific reviewers, e.g. Go SDK reviewer, for their approvals) and re-run CI.

testcase: Stuck SDK breaking change labels
query:
title: Stuck SDK breaking change labels

question: [Microsoft.NetworkCloud 2026-07-01 API by bryan-strassner · Pull Request #43718 · Azure/azure-rest-a…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/43718) Should the SDK breaking change labels have gone away with the SDK CI all passing now? Or are they automatic as a result of the cross-version breaking change failure?
ground_truth:
The `BreakingChange-*-Sdk` labels are driven by automated diff/breaking-change detection, not by whether the SDK CI builds happen to pass — so they can remain even when all SDK validation jobs are green. The labels need to be addressed by adding a `sdk-suppressions.yaml`.

For NetworkCloud specifically, the per-language `-gen-result.html` reports were missing the expected `Absent SDK breaking changes suppressions` section. The cause is that generation is based on the **TypeSpec** project folder, but the existing suppression file lived under `resource-manager/`. The fix is to move (or copy) the existing suppression file from the `resource-manager/` location into the TypeSpec project folder so the generator picks it up; after moving the suppressions file, the analysis populates the expected sections and the appropriate labels can be added.

I opened a PR just renaming/rebranding RP name
[Rename resource provider AIGrounding to WebIQ by nader-m-elhadedy · Pull Request #28858 · Azure/azu…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/28858)
 
Tejaswi Salaigari following up on this thread [Nader Elhadedy: PR Review Needed | API Spec Review](https://teams.microsoft.com/l/message/19:0351f5f9404446e4b4fd4eaf2c27448d@thread.skype/1779102268135?tenantId=72f988bf-86f1-41af-91ab-2d7cd011db47&groupId=3e17dcb0-4257-4a30-b843-77f47f1d4121&parentMessageId=1779102268135&teamName=Azure%20SDK&channelName=API%20Spec%20Review&createdTime=1779102268135)
For this PR, I don't think we need ARMModelingReview since it's just renaming RP with same everything, so please provide the signoff label.
There are also some failing checks related to arm lease, as I updated lease.yaml file in my PR, so please tell me if you can bypass these checks or you have to make this change on your own.
 
cc: Mike Harder
Thanks
ground_truth:
Do not add or modify the `lease.yaml` file yourself — only ARM Modeling reviewers have access to add and delete lease files. Revert any lease.yaml changes in the PR. The Modeling team will add and delete the lease files on the author's behalf (e.g., via a separate PR like [#43850](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/43850) which adds ARM leases for partner PRs).

ground_truth:
The failure is because `@azure-tools/typespec-azure-portal-core` is missing from `emitter-package.json` in the SDK repos. `tsp-client` only uses `emitter-package.json` from the SDK repos for code generation; it does not resolve unknown dependencies from the spec repo's `package.json`. Even though the package is referenced in the spec repo's `package.json`, it needs to be explicitly added to `emitter-package.json` in each SDK repo to unblock SDK generation. This package contains portal metadata decorators and is treated like any other Azure TypeSpec package. The fix has been added in the public `azure-sdk-for-go` repo and needs to be synced to the `-pr` repo.

testcase: Mutually exclusive checks failing for RPSaaSMaster PRs
query:
title: Mutually exclusive checks failing for RPSaaSMaster PRs

question: I am seeing mutually exclusive checks fail. If I fix one, the other fails because of the fix.
 
As per SDK JS validation check:
It expects the following properties to be present for the supercomputer and maintenance requests resource
[image]
 
If I add these properties, swagger ModelValidation fails because I added those properties:
[image]
 
PRs:

- [Update 3P tier names by meghasankhlecha · Pull Request #28770 · Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/28770)
- [Add ManagedServiceIdentityProperty to supercomputer resource by meghasankhlecha · Pull Request #288…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/pull/28833)

Re-running the checks does not mitigate the issue.
ground_truth:
The root cause is that `ArmResourcePatchAsync` is being used for `MaintenanceRequests.update` and `Supercomputers.update`, but this template is not supported by the JS emitter, and `ArmResourcePatch*` templates are not recommended per the [ARM Resource Operations guidance](https://azure.github.io/typespec-azure/docs/howtos/arm/resource-operations/#resource-update-operations-patch). Update the spec to use `ArmCustomPatchAsync` for both operations so TypeSpec generates the correct update models and JS SDK code is produced properly:

```
interface MaintenanceRequests {
  update is ArmCustomPatchAsync<
    MaintenanceRequest,
    PatchModel = Azure.ResourceManager.Foundations.ResourceUpdateModel<
      MaintenanceRequest,
      MaintenanceRequestProperties
    >
  >;
}

interface Supercomputers {
  update is ArmCustomPatchAsync<
    Supercomputer,
    PatchModel = Azure.ResourceManager.Foundations.ResourceUpdateModel<
      Supercomputer,
      SupercomputerProperties
    >
  >;
}
```

Note that ModelValidation typically fails when the spec changes but the associated examples are not updated in sync — spec and examples must be updated together.

testcase: ACS Identity 2026-07-22 — retirement compliance version bump, TypeSpec conversion
query:
title: ACS Identity 2026-07-22 — retirement compliance version bump, TypeSpec conversion

question: PR #43948 — ACS Identity stable 2026-07-22

Link: [[IC3 ACS - Auth] New API version for ACS Identity… by JohnRFraser · Pull Reques…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/43948)

Hi, looking for guidance on the right process for this PR. It's a new stable version for ACS Identity created solely for retirement compliance — zero API surface changes from the prior approved version ([IC3 ACS - Auth] ACS Token Exchange for Teams Phone Extensibility by paveldostal…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/34712)). The spec was converted from hand-written swagger to TypeSpec to meet the TypeSpec Requirement CI check.

Two labels are blocking merge:

- new-api-version — is API stewardship review required for a retirement compliance version bump with no API changes?
- BreakingChangeReviewRequired — cross-version check flags structural TypeSpec conversion differences (not behavioral)

What's the appropriate path here?
ground_truth:
Do not convert to TypeSpec and add a new API version in the same PR — this is a "diagonal conversion," and the spec PR checks are not designed to handle it. Instead, split the work into two PRs: first convert your latest existing API version from swagger to TypeSpec (a horizontal conversion that introduces no API changes and no version revving), then add the new API version in TypeSpec in a second PR. The TypeSpec conversion PR may require special approvals for the expected failing checks (parameter ordering, inline `$ref`s, api-version parameter location, etc.), which are structural artifacts of TypeSpec-generated swagger rather than behavioral changes; the team has handled many same-version TSP conversions. The second PR that adds the new TypeSpec API version should then be very simple.

Converting is required even though the version is being created only for retirement compliance, because SDKs can now be generated only from TypeSpec (a hard requirement). After splitting, the conversion-only PR should follow the standard data-plane TypeSpec conversion review path; raise the breaking-change/cross-version failures with the reviewers so they can confirm the differences are non-behavioral conversion artifacts.

---

testcase: ARM Review Needed – SKU Resources PR (Feedback Addressed)
query:
title: ARM Review Needed – SKU Resources PR (Feedback Addressed)

question: Hi,

We are trying to merge our PR for adding SKU resources and have already addressed all feedback from the previous reviewers.
We're encountering an issue where the `WaitForARMFeedback` label is automatically removed by GitHub actions, preventing us from progressing.
This is time-sensitive, and our partners are currently blocked waiting for this documentation.

It would be greatly appreciated if the review and approval could be expedited.
Thanks!
[[Microsoft.Marketplace] - add skus resource 2025 by meirloichter · Pull Request #42753 · Azure/azur…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs/pull/42753)
ground_truth:
The `WaitForARMFeedback` label is automation-managed, so re-adding it manually will not stick — its removal is a symptom of the PR's current review state, not the root problem. The actual blocker is that the PR still has `ARMChangesRequested` and does not yet have `ARMSignedOff`. The fix is to remove the `ARMChangesRequested` label once all feedback is addressed (this requirement should have been documented in the "Next steps to merge" comment on the PR), then ask the ARM reviewer to re-check and clear the review state.

If the review is time-sensitive, follow the ARM review escalation path (Get your PR reviewed by ARM). Once ARM clears the review and adds `ARMSignedOff`, obtain the final approving review from someone with write access and merge.

---

testcase: API PR Build failure for multiple tags
query:
title: API PR Build failure for multiple tags

question: Hi all, I've raised this PR in the private repo for API review but it's encountering the following error
[Add attribute namespace entity to microsoft.AuthorizationAdd updates · Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-p…](https://github.com/Azure/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/actions/runs/21880714493/job/63162103774)
 
```
Error: Multiple input-file definitions for tag package-2020-10-01 in /home/runner/work/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/azure-rest-api-specs-pr/before/specification/authorization/resource-manager/readme.md
```

I have removed the duplicated section in readme.md that's reported but it continues getting flagged. Can I get some help on how to resolve/suppress this?
ground_truth: We identified that the build failure was caused by an incorrect reference path in the new entity’s swagger file: the path to types.json was different between the public and private repositories. This caused the validator to fail when resolving the referenced file. We fixed the issue by updating the swagger reference to use the correct path that matches the target repository layout.
